title: TF-Storage
description: A lightweight, high-capacity storage mod for Minecraft 1.12.2. Refactored from Ender Utilities.
body:
# TF Storage

> **⚠️ Notice**
>
> This project is a **derivative work** based on [Ender Utilities](https://github.com/maruohon/enderutilities).
>
> Original code is Copyright (C) maruohon and contributors.
> Refactored and modified by **TinyFish3714**.

---

## 📖 Introduction

**TF Storage** is a Minecraft mod dedicated to **high-capacity and convenient storage** solutions.

Addressing the chaos of vanilla inventory management, TF Storage strips away the bloat to focus entirely on the core storage experience. Born from a thorough refactor, it delivers a lightweight and efficient way to manage your massive collection of resources.

## Core Features

**TF Bag** (formerly Handy Bag)  
![TFBag](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/eaa0205cb4ea0b560faffe171d68a9cd60286322_0.webp)  
**By default, opening the inventory also opens the TF pack, while sneaking only opens the inventory. This can be reversed through configuration, so that opening the inventory while sneaking also opens the TF pack. Opening the inventory directly will not open the TF pack.**
1. Offers extra storage that feels almost identical to the vanilla inventory.
2. Provides four storage slots that can be switched freely.
3. Stack limits vary depending on the inserted TF Unit (formerly memory card).
4. Upgrades 2×2 crafting to 3×3 crafting.
5. Includes inventory-sorting and multiple hot-key operations for effortless management.
6. Like the original Handy Bag, can be configured to open only while sneaking.
7. TF Bag adds 27 slots; TF Bag (Large) adds 55 slots.
8. Adds **Restock Mode** and **Pickup Mode**.  
   - **Restock Mode**: automatically replenishes blocks when the stack in your hand runs out or replaces broken tools with fresh ones from the bag.  
   - **Pickup Mode**: toggles between three states—OFF, MATCH, and ALL.  
     - **MATCH**: only collects items that already exist somewhere inside the bag.  
     - **ALL**: sweeps every dropped item into the bag’s storage, regardless of whether it is already present.
9. Baubles compatibility: when Baubles is present, open the Baubles screen directly from the TFBag GUI and return; the bag automatically gains a bauble tag, letting you equip it in a bauble slot and free inventory space.
10. JEI integration: with JEI loaded, transfer recipes straight into the **TF Bag’s built-in crafting matrix**—no external workbench required.
11. FTB Sidebar support: the original Handy Bag couldn’t display FTB-mod buttons on its left panel; this mod adds full compatibility so the UI feels closer to vanilla.

**TF Chest** (formerly Handy Chest)  
![TF Chest](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/bb1b55d3984205f92a4624b9d9df68dd3a4c569c.png)  
1. Larger storage than vanilla chests.  
2. Four switchable storage slots, just like TF Bag.  
3. Stack limits depend on the inserted TF Unit.  
4. Inventory-sorting and quick shortcuts for easy management.  
5. Four tiers: Small, Normal, Large, Extended—offering 27, 45, 72, and 108 slots respectively (Large & Extended show the same visible grid; extra slots are accessed via scrollbar).  
6. Supports vanilla comparators, hoppers, and automation pipes from other mods.

**TF Unit** (formerly memory card)  
* Four tiers—6B, 8B, 10B, 12B—raising the per-slot stack limit to 64, 256, 1 024, and 4 096 items respectively.

## 🗓️ Future Plans

We are currently focusing on the stability and optimization of the 1.12.2 version.
Plans are in place to migrate to modern Minecraft versions (e.g., 1.20+) in the future. Stay tuned!
